title: spicy juicy kenyan chestnut mushroom bowl
description: A hearty, spicy, and juicy dish featuring blanched chestnut mushrooms, onions, bell peppers, and a blend of Kenyan spices. Perfect for a lazy Sunday meal.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the finely chopped red bell pepper and cook for 2 minutes, until tender.
Add the chestnut mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the tomato paste, curry powder, garam masala, cumin powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro, if desired.
Blanch the chestnut mushrooms in boiling water for 2-3 minutes to remove any impurities and make them tender.
Drain the mushrooms and set them aside to cool.
Once cooled, slice the mushrooms into thick strips and set them aside until needed.
Blanching the mushrooms is an important step to remove any impurities and make them tender.
It also helps to bring out their natural sweetness.
This step can be skipped if you prefer a more rustic texture in your dish.
